raw_output_payload.content: while IFS= read -r p; do mkdir -p "$(dirname "$p")"; done < /workspace/files.txt
raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need to read file paths, get unique, make directories. Use bash: cat file | sort -u | xargs -I{} mkdir -p "$(dirname "{}")". But need to handle spaces. Use while read -r line. So: while IFS= read -r path; do mkdir -p "$(dirname "$path")"; done < /workspace/files.txt. Output single line command. Use bash -c? Just one line: while IFS= read -r p; do mkdir -p "$(dirname "$p")"; done < /workspace/files.txt.
